title: Division by zero in CASE statement that should avoid it
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table(id INTEGER, value INTEGER, description VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 10, 'Valid entry'), (2, 0, 'Division by zero'), (3, NULL, 'Null value');
SELECT CASE WHEN value IS NOT NULL AND value > 0 THEN id / value ELSE -1 END AS result FROM test_table;
SELECT CASE WHEN value IS NOT NULL AND value > 0 THEN 1.0 * id / value ELSE -1 END AS result_float FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE another_table(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, ref_id INTEGER REFERENCES test_table(id));
key_bug_features: Division by zero in CASE statement evaluation
